results.possibleIdentity: Dr. Phillip S. Mittelman (1925–1999) was a prominent nuclear physicist and computer scientist who founded Mathematical Applications Group, Inc. (MAGI) in 1966. Originally based in Elmsford and White Plains, New York, MAGI initially specialized in nuclear radiation shielding simulations using Monte Carlo ray-tracing techniques. Under Mittelman's leadership, the company adapted this software into 'SynthaVision,' one of the first commercial systems for 3D computer animation using constructive solid geometry (CSG) and ray tracing. This technology famously provided the majority of the CGI for the 1982 Disney film 'Tron,' specifically for sequences involving light cycles, tanks, and recognizers. Mittelman's work spanned a wide array of technical and creative fields including mathematics, physics, and computer graphics. His multidisciplinary approach and the timing of his career align with the activities of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, which promoted collaborations between scientists and artists. MAGI also created early computer-generated advertisements for companies like IBM, which is headquartered in nearby Armonk, New York. He held degrees from Harvard University and a Ph.D. from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.
